_legend.purpose: Task graph for the TinyTalk history/usage feature, topologically sorted for parallel execution. Scaffold only — titles/deps/order are load-bearing; full spec bodies are authored later.
_legend.hierarchy: prd -> requirement (one per scope A/C/D/B) -> spec (leaf; each spec = one sub-issue = one commit per AGENTS.md).

node_fields.depends_on: hard predecessors (must be merged first); drives the topo sort
node_fields.wave: parallel batch index within its phase
node_fields.touches: files this node edits — nodes in the same wave MUST touch disjoint files

_legend.parallelism_rule: Everything in the same wave has disjoint `touches` and satisfied deps, so it runs concurrently (separate agents / worktrees). Across waves is sequential.

serialization_notes: tinytalk/history.py: created by A1, extended by B1 -> B1 waits for A1 (different waves).
tinytalk/cli.py is the one shared spine among consumers: A3 -> C1 -> C2 are serialized by wave to avoid merge conflicts.
Disjoint files enable the real parallelism: A1||A2 (wave 1), A3||B1 (wave 2), C1||D1 (wave 3).

nodes.title: PRD: command history + usage tracking
nodes.summary: Capture every prompt outcome to a dated-JSONL store under XDG_STATE_HOME; user audits & reuses past commands; foundation for future eval. Lean persistence = no big/sensitive blobs, all structured metadata kept.

nodes.title: Requirement A — Foundation: capture seam, store, faithful usage
nodes.summary: In-memory capture record built at 4 hook points; one thin sink; dated-JSONL storage; usage plumbed faithfully through failures and retries. No sink framework.

nodes.title: Requirement C — `tt history` viewer
nodes.summary: Browse/audit records; fzf-first with full-record preview, plaintext numbered fallback; store-all, dedup-the-view (exact-normalized); selecting yields the command.

nodes.title: Requirement D — Prompt-mode ↑/↓ command recall
nodes.summary: Inside AI mode, ↑/↓ recall past commands verbatim into the buffer (Atuin model); exits AI mode ready to run; restores default arrows on leave.

nodes.title: Requirement B — Retention & rotation
nodes.summary: Delete day-segments >7 days old; 15 MB total safety trim; prune only touches old files (no line-rewrite, no lock, no lost-append).

nodes.title: History store & record model
nodes.summary: default_state_dir() (XDG_STATE_HOME, mirrors default_cache_dir); dated JSONL segments YYYY-MM-DD.jsonl; atomic best-effort append (0600, swallow OSError); record (de)serialize; read_recent(n) API; exact-normalized view-dedup helper (reuse cache_key); monotonic ids across segments.
nodes.done_when: records round-trip through the store; read_recent returns newest-first; ids monotonic across a segment boundary.

nodes.title: Faithful usage & cost plumbing
nodes.summary: Lift _cost into tinytalk/cost.py (shared by eval + history) + add cost_breakdown(usage,price) producer; add usage to NoValidCommand and populate all 4 raise sites; accumulate per-attempt usage + latency in engine.generate so retried successes and failures report full spend; add prompt_surface_hash to TierResult; POPULATE the per-attempt attempts_detail ledger (engine yields per-format-attempt entries, controller tags tier+backend, carried on TierResult and the terminal FormatError/NoValidCommand).
nodes.done_when: a forced escalation/failure reports non-zero tokens+cost; cost_breakdown four buckets sum to cost; engine.generate sums usage across a first-format-fail-then-success; attempts_detail has one entry per attempt tagged with tier+backend; TierResult carries prompt_surface_hash; eval+report still green after cost.py extraction.

nodes.title: Capture hooks + sink wiring + recall porcelain
nodes.summary: Build the capture record at the 3 write sites in cli._run (4 outcome states); resolve model via config.backend(name).model; compute cost_usd + cost_breakdown via cost.py; copy prompt_surface_hash off the result; set billable per the pinned rule; enrich attempts_detail entries with model + per-attempt cost_usd; write via A1 sink on ALL outcomes (ok / cache_hit / no_command / transport_error); ConfigError writes no record. Add the machine-readable recall command (porcelain, DEDUPED) the widget shells out to.
nodes.done_when: every tt invocation on the request path appends exactly one faithful record across the taxonomy states; a capture-side OSError never changes stdout/stderr/exit; billable follows the pinned rule; attempts_detail is populated with per-attempt model+cost; porcelain emits DEDUPED recent commands NUL-delimited (empty store prints nothing, exit 0).

nodes.title: Retention sweep
nodes.summary: On write, prune day-segments older than 7 days (unlink) and enforce a 15 MB total safety cap; cheap first-line/mtime checks; never rewrite the active segment.
nodes.done_when: segments >7d removed; total bounded ≤15 MB; concurrent appends never lost.

nodes.title: `tt history` command + view-dedup + plaintext fallback
nodes.summary: New `history` branch in cli.main; query via read_recent; collapse duplicates in the VIEW only (store-all preserved); plaintext numbered listing (id, time, prompt→command, cost) when fzf absent.
nodes.done_when: `tt history` lists recent newest-first, dupes collapsed in view, plaintext path works with no fzf.

nodes.title: Prompt-mode ↑/↓ recall widget
nodes.summary: Bind up/down inside _TT_AI_MODE to a recall widget; on first press shell out once to the A3 porcelain and cache the array; ↑/↓ walk deduped past commands into BUFFER; leaving AI mode restores default arrow bindings.
nodes.done_when: verified via .claude/skills/test-shell-ui (tmux + stubbed tt): ↑/↓ walks past commands; exit restores normal arrows.

nodes.title: fzf interactive picker + preview
nodes.summary: fzf-first picker over records with a preview pane showing the full record (prompt, command, explanation, model, tokens, cost, time); selection prints the command (copy/stdout). Falls back to C1's plaintext when fzf missing.
nodes.done_when: fzf present → interactive pick with preview; fzf absent → C1 fallback; selection emits the command.
